search_queries_used: site:doh.gov.ae well-child visit growth screening standard Abu Dhabi
candidate_sources_rejected: EHS Child Health and Vaccinations service page
rejection_reasons: The EHS page is an operational service summary.
The DoH weight-management service is not an exact pediatric faltering-growth assessment source.
population_applicability: Partial: DoH covers growth screening from 1 month to 6 years and NICE covers children with weight loss or faltering growth; neither covers every short-stature or chronic-disease cause in the broad baseline.
setting_applicability: Partial: sources cover Abu Dhabi well-child screening and England primary, community, and specialist care.
UAE_applicability: Direct for DoH well-child growth screening in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates; NICE faltering-growth assessment is international support requiring UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: DoH V10 official revised copy and current NICE NG75 recommendations page opened on 2026-07-11.
superseded_check: Current official DoH revised copy and NICE recommendations page checked; no newer replacement identified on those official pages.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports the age-limited UAE growth-screening and physical-assessment context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines the 1-month-to-6-year population and requires parent history, age-specific physical examination, vital signs, developmental milestones, screening, vaccination discussion, and EMR documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped weight, length or height, head circumference, growth-chart, feeding-history, and prior-record documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies age-linked weight, length or height, head circumference, growth plotting, feeding and medical history, developmental milestones, screening, immunization review, and caregiver counselling.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped growth concern, weight loss, measurement, feeding, appetite, developmental, social, and clinical-assessment items.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies weight and height measurement, clinical/developmental/social assessment, detailed feeding or eating history, observation, and assessment-directed investigations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped feeding and nutrition-history documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies suckling, bottle feeding, routines, environment, aversion, interaction, appetite, food types, and feeding-diary domains.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped monitoring, worsening concern, and clinician-arranged referral documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines growth monitoring and referral for underlying disorder, failure to respond, slow growth, rapid weight loss, severe undernutrition, safeguarding concern, or acute illness.

unresolved_source_gaps: Short stature, broad chronic disease, respiratory negatives, and the legacy failure-to-thrive diagnosis label are not fully supported by the reviewed sections.
The DoH standard has an effective date of TBD and an age limit of 6 years.
Fixed follow-up intervals and generic normal findings remain unsupported.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
